description: Phase 12: Habits & Bloodwork Verify & Polish — Verify streak logic, kegel timer, bloodwork AI interpretation, dashboard integration, and edge cases

userStories.title: Verify habit creation, toggle, and streak calculation
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/features/habits/presentation/habit_provider.dart first
Read lib/features/habits/data/habit_repository.dart
4 preset habits display on HabitsScreen: porn_free, kegels, sleep_target, steps_target
Tapping a habit toggle calls logHabitDay() and updates UI optimistically
Streak recalculation runs after each log: walks backwards counting consecutive completed days
current_streak_days resets to 0 when a day is skipped (not logged or completed=false)
longest_streak_days only increases, never decreases
Last 30-day grid shows green for completed, grey for missed, ring for today
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Verify on Android device
userStories.technicalNotes: Streak calc is client-side in habit_repository.dart. Verify the algorithm handles timezone edge cases (user logs at 11:59 PM vs 12:01 AM). Check UNIQUE(profile_id, habit_type, log_date) constraint prevents duplicate logs.

userStories.title: Verify custom habit creation and soft delete
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/features/habits/presentation/habits_screen.dart
User can create custom habit with free-text name
Custom habit gets auto-generated unique type key (e.g., custom_timestamp)
Custom habit appears in the habits list alongside presets
Deleting a habit sets is_active=false (soft delete), habit disappears from UI
Soft-deleted habits do NOT appear on reload
Streak data preserved in DB after soft delete (no cascade)
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Check habit_entity.dart for the type key generation pattern. Verify is_active filter in repository queries.

userStories.title: Verify streak milestone dialog fires at 7, 30, 90, 180 days
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/features/habits/presentation/widgets/streak_milestone_dialog.dart
When current_streak_days reaches 7, milestone dialog shown with habit name and count
Same for 30, 90, and 180 day milestones
Dialog dismisses on tap and does not reappear same session
Milestone only fires once per threshold (not on every toggle after 7)
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Check how milestone state is tracked — should compare previous streak vs new streak to detect threshold crossing.

userStories.title: Verify kegel timer with all 3 protocols
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/features/habits/presentation/kegel_timer_provider.dart
Read lib/features/habits/presentation/kegel_timer_screen.dart
Quick Flicks protocol: 3 sets x 10 reps, 1s squeeze/relax, 10s rest between sets
Long Holds protocol: 3 sets x 5 reps, 5s squeeze/relax, 15s rest between sets
Reverse Kegels protocol: 3 sets x 5 reps, 5s squeeze/relax, 15s rest between sets
Phase transitions: squeeze -> relax -> next rep OR rest -> next set OR complete
Circular progress ring updates every second
Vibration feedback fires on each phase transition
Session completion auto-logs kegels habit via logHabitDay()
Timer can be paused and resumed
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Verify on Android device (vibration requires real device)
userStories.technicalNotes: Auto-logging is best-effort (errors suppressed). Verify the habitProvider is accessible from kegelTimerProvider. Check kegel_protocol.dart for protocol definitions.

userStories.title: Verify bloodwork logging with reference ranges and out-of-range detection
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/features/bloodwork/presentation/bloodwork_screen.dart
Read lib/features/bloodwork/data/bloodwork_repository.dart
12+ pre-defined test types organized in 4 tabs: Hormones, Metabolic, Cardiovascular, Vitamins
Adding a result with value outside reference range shows red indicator (is_out_of_range=true)
Adding a result within range shows green indicator
Adding a result within 10% of boundary shows amber borderline indicator
Custom test name accepted (free-text) with user-specified reference range
Edit and delete work correctly, with confirmation dialog on delete
is_out_of_range is a Postgres GENERATED ALWAYS column (read-only from client)
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Read bloodwork_entity.dart for isBorderline helper. Check bloodwork_test_types.dart for the 12 pre-defined types. Verify the computed column formula matches the client-side display logic.

userStories.title: Verify bloodwork detail screen with trend chart
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/features/bloodwork/presentation/bloodwork_detail_screen.dart
Navigate to /bloodwork/:testName shows trend chart for that test
Chart plots all historical values for the selected test (x=date, y=value)
Reference range shown as shaded band on chart
Out-of-range data points highlighted in red
Empty state shown if no history for selected test
Back navigation works correctly
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Uses FL Chart for rendering. Check if the chart handles single data point gracefully (no crash on 1 entry).

userStories.title: Verify AI bloodwork interpretation with consent check
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/features/bloodwork/presentation/bloodwork_provider.dart lines 224-399
AI interpretation only triggers if ai_consent_bloodwork=true on user profile
If consent=false, show appropriate message prompting user to enable in settings
AI call sends out-of-range + borderline results to ai_orchestrator_service.orchestrate()
Workflow type is 'interpret_bloodwork'
AI response parsed: interpretation text, possible_considerations array, consultation note
Response displayed in AiInterpretationCard with medical disclaimer
If AI call fails, deterministic fallback summary generated from rules
3-second debounce prevents rapid re-calls
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Consent is checked via profile repository getAiConsentBloodwork(). The debounce uses DateTime-based guard. Fallback builds human-readable summary from out-of-range patterns.

userStories.title: Verify habits and bloodwork surface on main dashboard
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/features/dashboard/presentation/dashboard_screen.dart
Read lib/features/bloodwork/presentation/widgets/bloodwork_summary_card.dart
Dashboard shows bloodwork summary card with count of flagged (out-of-range) results
Tapping bloodwork card navigates to /bloodwork
Dashboard shows habit streak progress for active habits
Tapping habits card navigates to /habits
Cards handle empty state gracefully (no habits logged, no bloodwork)
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Check how dashboard_provider.dart loads habits and bloodwork state. Verify providers are invalidated after mutations (Phase 13 wired this).

userStories.title: Verify RLS policies for habits and bloodwork tables
acceptanceCriteria: Read supabase/migrations for wt_habit_streaks, wt_habit_logs, wt_bloodwork_results
User can only SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE their own habit_streaks (via profile_id -> user_id)
User can only SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE their own habit_logs
User can only SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE their own bloodwork_results
Attempting to access another user's data returns empty result (not error)
Indexes exist: idx_habit_streaks_profile_active, idx_habit_logs_profile_type_date, idx_bloodwork_profile_test_date, idx_bloodwork_out_of_range
userStories.technicalNotes: RLS defined in migrations 20260220000008 and 20260309000002. Verify policies use the pattern: profile_id IN (SELECT id FROM wt_profiles WHERE user_id = auth.uid()).

userStories.title: Verify sensitive data handling for bloodwork
acceptanceCriteria: Read bloodwork_entity.dart is_sensitive field
Results marked is_sensitive=true are excluded from any data export
Sensitive results never included in AI context unless explicit consent given
Sensitive flag can be toggled per result
No sensitive data appears in debug logs or console output
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Check if the AI interpretation context builder filters out is_sensitive=true results when consent is partial. Verify no print() statements leak sensitive values.
